title: BCD-watch: Changes Report (weekly)
description: A feed that summarizes changes to BCD weekly

items.content_html:

BCD Metadata Changes: 24

Show all 24 changes

Note: The following represent changes to BCD metadata keys in the reported time period. Keys are not implementations, and they don't represent commitments toward implementation, etc. A new key occurring is perhaps a signal that some implementer was serious enough to add the key. Many of the features that are 'removed' are usually just 'moved' or 'renamed', but we don't have great ways to highlight that.

New entries (22)

The entries in this section were not found in BCD on Friday, 13 February 2026, but are now present. These usually do not have any support information attached to them, and often have no corresponding MDN page. Sometimes, entries that appear here also appear in the next section (“Removed BCD entries”) because of a Working Group deciding on a rename.

API

CSS

  1. css ➞ properties ➞ display ➞ grid-lanes
  2. css ➞ properties ➞ display ➞ inline-grid-lanes
  3. css ➞ properties ➞ flow-tolerance
  4. css ➞ properties ➞ flow-tolerance ➞ infinite

  6. css ➞ types ➞ random ➞ dashed-ident
  7. css ➞ types ➞ random ➞ element-shared
  8. css ➞ types ➞ random ➞ step

JAVASCRIPT

  1. javascript ➞ builtins ➞ RegExp ➞ compile ➞ rexexp_legacy_features

Removed entries (2)

The entries in this section were found in BCD on Friday, 13 February 2026, but are no longer present. The reasons for this removal can be anything from an experimental proposal that was never supported to a property or value being renamed to something else.

HTML

  1. html ➞ global_attributes ➞ nonce ➞ nonce_hiding

HTTP

  1. http ➞ headers ➞ X-Frame-Options ➞ ALLOW-FROM
  • Previous report: Monday, 16 February 2026

BCD Watch: A service of

This report was generated by diffing v7.3.2 (Friday, 13 February 2026) and v7.3.3 (Friday, 20 February 2026)

Show all 201 changes

Note: The following represent changes to BCD metadata keys in the reported time period. Keys are not implementations, and they don't represent commitments toward implementation, etc. A new key occurring is perhaps a signal that some implementer was serious enough to add the key. Many of the features that are 'removed' are usually just 'moved' or 'renamed', but we don't have great ways to highlight that.

Backfilled entries (86)

The entries in this section saw support information change since the report on Friday, 6 February 2026, but all the supported browser versions given were more than a couple of months old. Typically, these are cases of support information being updated long after the support actually landed in a browser, but nobody ever updated the BCD entry.
